component_candidates.ambiguity_note: This amount is for the drug-loaded optimized formulations (F2, F4, F6, F8).
This amount is for all PLGA-based optimized formulations (F1-F6).
This amount is for all PLGA-based optimized formulations (F1-F6).
This amount is for all PLGA-based optimized formulations (F1-F6).
This amount is for all PCL-based optimized formulations (F7-F8).
This concentration is for all optimized formulations (F1-F8).

relation_hints.note: F2 is a drug-loaded variant of F1 (empty).
F4 is a drug-loaded variant of F3 (empty).
F6 is a drug-loaded variant of F5 (empty).
F8 is a drug-loaded variant of F7 (empty).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Etoposide-loaded nanoparticles were prepared using nanoprecipitation and emulsion solvent evaporation techniques using polylactide-co-glycolic acid and poly(ε-caprolactone) in presence of Pluronic F68, respectively.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Etoposide-loaded nanoparticles were prepared using nanoprecipitation and emulsion solvent evaporation techniques using polylactide-co-glycolic acid and poly(ε-caprolactone) in presence of Pluronic F68, respectively.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Effect of formulation variables like stabilizer concentration, amount of polymer, and drug was studied.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Effect of formulation variables like stabilizer concentration, amount of polymer, and drug was studied.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: These parameters were found to affect particle size, zeta potential, drug content, and entrapment efﬁciency of nanoparti- cles.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: These parameters were found to affect particle size, zeta potential, drug content, and entrapment efﬁciency of nanoparti- cles.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Polylactide-co-glycolide 50/50 (PLGA 50/50, molecular weight of 10,000), poly(ε-caprolactone) (PCL) (molecular weight of 40,000), and Pluronic F 68 (F68) were procured from Sigma-Aldrich Chemicals, USA.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Polylactide-co-glycolide 50/50 (PLGA 50/50, molecular weight of 10,000), poly(ε-caprolactone) (PCL) (molecular weight of 40,000), and Pluronic F 68 (F68) were procured from Sigma-Aldrich Chemicals, USA.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: PLGA 75/25 (Purasorb PDLGA R 75/25, molecular weight of 10,000) and PLGA 85/15 (Purasorb PDLGA R 85/15, molecular weight of 10,000) were generously gifted by Purac, The Netherlands.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: PLGA 75/25 (Purasorb PDLGA R 75/25, molecular weight of 10,000) and PLGA 85/15 (Purasorb PDLGA R 85/15, molecular weight of 10,000) were generously gifted by Purac, The Netherlands.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Etoposide was gift from Dabur research foundation, Sahibabad, India.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Etoposide was gift from Dabur research foundation, Sahibabad, India.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Triple distilled water was used in the preparation of nanoparticles.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Triple distilled water was used in the preparation of nanoparticles.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: All other materials were of analytical grade (Spectrochem, Mumbai, India) and used as received.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: All other materials were of analytical grade (Spectrochem, Mumbai, India) and used as received.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Nanoparticles were prepared by nanoprecipitation (Fessi et al. 1989; Peltonen et al. 2002) and emulsion solvent evaporation methods (Leroux et al. 1995) using PLGA polymers and PCL, respectively.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Nanoparticles were prepared by nanoprecipitation (Fessi et al. 1989; Peltonen et al. 2002) and emulsion solvent evaporation methods (Leroux et al. 1995) using PLGA polymers and PCL, respectively.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The optimized formulation was prepared using nanoprecipitation method as follows: polymer (50 mg) and etoposide (5 mg) were dissolved in acetone.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The optimized formulation was prepared using nanoprecipitation method as follows: polymer (50 mg) and etoposide (5 mg) were dissolved in acetone.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Dichloromethane was used to dissolve PCL and etoposide in case of emulsion solvent evaporation method.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Dichloromethane was used to dissolve PCL and etoposide in case of emulsion solvent evaporation method.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Resulting organic phase was added slowly under moderate magnetic stirring into triple distilled wa- ter(TDW) containingF68asstabilizer(1.0%w/v).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Resulting organic phase was added slowly under moderate magnetic stirring into triple distilled wa- ter(TDW) containingF68asstabilizer(1.0%w/v).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: The organic solvent was then removed by evaporat- ing under magnetic stirring or under reduced pressure at 35◦C for approximately 1 hr (Rotavapor, Buchi, Switzerland).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: The organic solvent was then removed by evaporat- ing under magnetic stirring or under reduced pressure at 35◦C for approximately 1 hr (Rotavapor, Buchi, Switzerland).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Entire dis- persion was centrifuged at 14,000 rpm at 25◦C for 10 min (Cool- ing Compufuge, Remi, Mumbai) in three cycles.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Entire dis- persion was centrifuged at 14,000 rpm at 25◦C for 10 min (Cool- ing Compufuge, Remi, Mumbai) in three cycles.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Supernatant was analyzed for free drug content and the sediment constituting nanoparticles was freeze-dried.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Supernatant was analyzed for free drug content and the sediment constituting nanoparticles was freeze-dried.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: For freeze-drying, prefreezing of samples was done at –20◦C for 20 hr, then the ﬂasks were connected to freeze-drier (Maxi Dry Lyo, Heto, Germany) un- der vacuum (1 mbar, –110◦C). The process was continued till free-ﬂowing powder was obtained.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: For freeze-drying, prefreezing of samples was done at –20◦C for 20 hr, then the ﬂasks were connected to freeze-drier (Maxi Dry Lyo, Heto, Germany) un- der vacuum (1 mbar, –110◦C). The process was continued till free-ﬂowing powder was obtained.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Different formulation variables like polymer amount (25, 50, 100, and 200 mg), concentration of stabilizer (0.5, 0.75, 1.0, and 2.0% w/v), and etoposide amount (2.5, 5, 10, and 20 mg) were varied and the effect on size, zeta potential, and entrap- ment efﬁciency was studied. Only one parameter was changed in each series of experiments.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Different formulation variables like polymer amount (25, 50, 100, and 200 mg), concentration of stabilizer (0.5, 0.75, 1.0, and 2.0% w/v), and etoposide amount (2.5, 5, 10, and 20 mg) were varied and the effect on size, zeta potential, and entrap- ment efﬁciency was studied. Only one parameter was changed in each series of experiments.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Drug free nanoparticles were pre- pared by the same methods without the addition of etoposide and characterized.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Drug free nanoparticles were pre- pared by the same methods without the addition of etoposide and characterized.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Average particle diameter, polydispersity index, and surface potential for each batch of prepared nanoparticles were deter- mined by using Zetasizer 3000HS (Malvern Instruments, UK).
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Average particle diameter, polydispersity index, and surface potential for each batch of prepared nanoparticles were deter- mined by using Zetasizer 3000HS (Malvern Instruments, UK).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Values reported are the mean and standard deviation of three batches of nanoparticles.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Values reported are the mean and standard deviation of three batches of nanoparticles.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: To determine entrapment efﬁciency of nanoparticles, sediment formed after centrifugation was dissolved in acetonitrile and analyzed for etoposide.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: To determine entrapment efﬁciency of nanoparticles, sediment formed after centrifugation was dissolved in acetonitrile and analyzed for etoposide.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Drug content and encapsulation efﬁciency were calculated by follow- ing equations: Drug content (% w/w) = Mass of drug in nanoparticles / Mass of nanoparticles recovered × 100; Entrapment efﬁciency (%) = Amount of etoposide in sediment / Initial amount of etoposide taken × 100
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Drug content and encapsulation efﬁciency were calculated by follow- ing equations: Drug content (% w/w) = Mass of drug in nanoparticles / Mass of nanoparticles recovered × 100; Entrapment efﬁciency (%) = Amount of etoposide in sediment / Initial amount of etoposide taken × 100

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: On the basis of preliminary studies, amount of drug, amount of polymer, and concentration of stabilizer were optimized to 5 mg, 50 mg, and 1.0% w/v.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: On the basis of preliminary studies, amount of drug, amount of polymer, and concentration of stabilizer were optimized to 5 mg, 50 mg, and 1.0% w/v.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Formulation characters of the opti- mized formulations prepared with PLGA-copolymers and PCL are given in Table 1.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Formulation characters of the opti- mized formulations prepared with PLGA-copolymers and PCL are given in Table 1.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Table 1: Formulation characters for the optimized nanoparticle formulations
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Formulation characters for the optimized nanoparticle formulations
PLGA 50/50 (Mean ± SD) Empty
PLGA 50/50 (Mean ± SD) Drug loaded
PLGA 75/25 (Mean ± SD) Empty
PLGA 75/25 (Mean ± SD) Drug loaded
PLGA 85/15 (Mean ± SD) Empty
PLGA 85/15 (Mean ± SD) Drug loaded
PCL (Mean ± SD) Empty
PCL (Mean ± SD) Drug loaded
aPolydispersity index.
bZeta potential.
cEncapsulation efﬁciency; each value is mean of three independent determinations.

evidence_spans.supporting_text: polymer (50 mg) and etoposide (5 mg) were dissolved in acetone.
F68 as stabilizer (1.0% w/v).

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Dichloromethane was used to dissolve PCL and etoposide in case of emulsion solvent evaporation method.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Dichloromethane was used to dissolve PCL and etoposide in case of emulsion solvent evaporation method.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: volume of solvent was decided to 10 mL.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: volume of solvent was decided to 10 mL.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: Aqueous phase volume, thus, was optimized and maintained as 25 mL for all the preparations.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: Aqueous phase volume, thus, was optimized and maintained as 25 mL for all the preparations.

evidence_spans.source_locator_text: For optimized formulation, containing stabilizer 1.0%, PLGA 50/50 produced nanoparticles of 91.8 ± 0.74 nm, whereas at same stabilizer concentrations, PLGA 75/25 and PLGA 85/15 produced nanoparticles of size 103.7±0.18nm and 105.1±0.38nm, respectively. PCL leads to the formation of bigger particles (257.2 ± 0.96 nm) when compared with size of nanoparticles prepared by PLGA-copolymers.
evidence_spans.supporting_text: For optimized formulation, containing stabilizer 1.0%, PLGA 50/50 produced nanoparticles of 91.8 ± 0.74 nm, whereas at same stabilizer concentrations, PLGA 75/25 and PLGA 85/15 produced nanoparticles of size 103.7±0.18nm and 105.1±0.38nm, respectively. PCL leads to the formation of bigger particles (257.2 ± 0.96 nm) when compared with size of nanoparticles prepared by PLGA-copolymers.
